red_team.verdict: drift_validity MINOR_ISSUE -- reproducible but NARROWER than 'generalizes across held-out blocks'
red_team.what_it_shows: Monte-Carlo SUBSAMPLING stability on ONE Lee2019 cache at ONE soft covariate: all 4 blocks resample 30-of-54 subjects from the SAME 54-subject pool at session_auc~0.52 / overlap~0.989. NULL_cov=[5,1,5,3] all <=7 reproduces exactly; POS stable 17-20%.
red_team.what_it_does_NOT_show: NOT independent datasets, NOT distributional/covariate drift, NOT cross-site generalization. Part A varies only the RNG seed; the #1 caveat (covariate strength) is untouched here and REFUTED in the adverse direction by the strong-cov study (7.3%/26% breach). n=4 correlated points cannot bound the drift tail.
red_team.calibration_optimism: tau calibrated on the same 54-subject pool (80e6 calib NULL_cov->3; 90e6 held-out->5): mild calibration optimism; off-distribution transfer untested.
